title: Rising prevalence of multiple sclerosis worldwide: Insights from the Atlas of MS third edition
rationale: Topic 0A — Global epidemiology anchor. Atlas of MS 3rd edition covering 115 countries; establishes 2.8M worldwide prevalence and regional surveillance gaps. Most geographically representative seed in the corpus.

title: Global regional and national burden of multiple sclerosis 1990–2016: a systematic analysis for the Global Burden of Disease Study 2016
rationale: Topic 0B — GBD 2016 MS systematic burden analysis across 195 countries using Bayesian meta-regression; provides DALYs incidence and mortality with uncertainty intervals.

title: Multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 1A — Canonical Lancet Seminar overview. Most-cited field overview establishing core tension between inflammatory and neurodegenerative disease phases. Foundational conceptual anchor.

title: Diagnosis of multiple sclerosis: 2017 revisions of the McDonald Criteria
rationale: Topic 1B — Living diagnostic standard. Establishes the dissemination-in-space-and-time logic cited in essentially every clinical MS paper. Read before the 2024 revision.

title: Relapses and progression of disability in multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 1bA — Foundational natural history study. Lyon cohort of 1844 patients established that progressive disability proceeds independently of relapse activity. Paradigm-shifting pre-DMT baseline.

title: Natural history of multiple sclerosis: a unifying concept
rationale: Topic 1bB — Unifying natural history concept. RRMS/SPMS/PPMS as age-dependent manifestations of one process; disability milestones reached at similar ages regardless of initial phenotype.

title: Multiple sclerosis pathology
rationale: Topic 2A — Lassmann lesion biology review. Synthesizes lesion heterogeneity chronic active lesion biology and cortical pathology. Bridge between immunology and tissue damage.

title: Multiple sclerosis: an immune or neurodegenerative disorder?
rationale: Topic 2B — Trapp & Nave neurodegeneration framing. Argues axonal damage is primary not bystander; set the stage for the neurodegeneration-focused progressive MS research agenda.

title: Multiple sclerosis genomic map implicates peripheral immune cells and microglia in susceptibility
rationale: Topic 3A — Definitive GWAS mapping 200+ susceptibility loci. Confirmed HLA-DRB1*15:01 as dominant signal; identified T-cell B-cell and microglial biology as therapeutic targets.

title: Genetics of multiple sclerosis: an overview and new directions
rationale: Topic 3B — Genetic architecture interpretation. Accessible narrative of what GWAS landscape means biologically including polygenic architecture and Mendelian randomization findings.

title: Longitudinal analysis reveals high prevalence of Epstein-Barr virus associated with multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 4A — EBV causal evidence. 10M+ US military cohort; 32-fold increase in MS risk after EBV seroconversion with temporal ordering established. Strongest epidemiological evidence for causal trigger.

title: Environmental risk factors for multiple sclerosis. Part I: the role of infection
rationale: Topic 4B — Full environmental risk framework: EBV vitamin D latitude smoking sunlight. Cumulative epidemiological logic that made Bjornevik 2022 the capstone not an isolated finding.

title: 2021 MAGNIMS–CMSC–NAIMS consensus recommendations on the use of MRI in patients with multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 5A — MRI consensus standards. Current international consensus on MRI across full care arc covering lesion types spinal cord imaging brain volume and emerging markers.

title: Association of chronic active multiple sclerosis lesions with disability in vivo
rationale: Topic 5B — Paramagnetic rim lesions in vivo. Key paper establishing clinical relevance of PRL for disability accumulation; now embedded in 2024 McDonald criteria update.

title: Neurofilaments as biomarkers in neurological disorders
rationale: Topic 6A — NfL biological foundation. Foundational review on neurofilament light chain establishing biology assay methodology and interpretive framework across neurological diseases.

title: The potential of serum neurofilament as biomarker for multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 6B — Blood biomarkers in clinical practice. Translational review on NfL and GFAP use in clinical MS practice for treatment monitoring prognosis and individual patient tracking.

title: Ocrelizumab versus interferon beta-1a in relapsing multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 7A — OPERA I/II trials cementing B-cell depletion as dominant RRMS treatment paradigm. Represents mechanistic shift away from T-cell-centric thinking.

title: Ocrelizumab versus placebo in primary progressive multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 7B — ORATORIO trial — first drug to demonstrate efficacy in PPMS. Watershed moment proving progressive MS is not completely insulated from anti-inflammatory intervention.

title: Defining the clinical course of multiple sclerosis: the 2013 revisions
rationale: Topic 8A — 2013 phenotype revision introducing activity and progression as orthogonal axes. Formally defined PIRA as distinct construct reshaping trial design and clinical monitoring.

title: Smouldering multiple sclerosis: the real MS
rationale: Topic 8B — Smoldering disease argument. Synthesizes evidence that low-grade diffuse CNS inflammation drives insidious worsening even in clinically stable patients.

title: Patient-reported outcomes in multiple sclerosis care
rationale: Topic 9A — PRO landscape in MS. Establishes that PROs are selected without clear justification and few have adequate psychometric quality; frames the problem space clearly.

title: Evaluation of multiple sclerosis disability outcome measures using pooled clinical trial data
rationale: Topic 9B — MSOAC pooled database of 12776 trial participants evaluating EDSS head-to-head with competing outcomes. Proposes and validates multidimensional disability battery.

title: The incidence and prevalence of psychiatric disorders in multiple sclerosis: a systematic review
rationale: Topic 10A — Comorbidity prevalence. Systematic review establishing depression anxiety and bipolar disorder prevalence in MS — substantially higher than general population and underdiagnosed.

title: Cognitive impairment in multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 10B — Cognitive impairment as core feature affecting 40–65% of people with MS. Foundational paper for cognitive MS research and for understanding why EDSS misrepresents disease burden.

title: Rate of pregnancy-related relapse in multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 11A — PRIMS landmark study. Established natural history across pregnancy cycle: decreased relapse third trimester then post-partum rebound. Shapes every pregnancy management guideline.

title: Family planning considerations in people with multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 11B — Current comprehensive review of MS and family planning covering all DMT classes washout periods rebound risk breastfeeding and tension between maternal disease control and fetal safety.

title: International Pediatric Multiple Sclerosis Study Group criteria for pediatric multiple sclerosis and immune-mediated central nervous system demyelinating disorders: revisions to the 2007 definitions
rationale: Topic 12A — Authoritative pediatric diagnostic criteria. Establishes how ADEM MOGAD and NMOSD are distinguished from MS in children — clinically critical differential with different prognoses.

title: Trial of fingolimod versus interferon beta-1a in pediatric multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 12B — PARADIGMS trial — first major RCT in pediatric MS. Established fingolimod superiority and opened regulatory pathways for DMT approval in pediatric populations.

title: Embodiment of structural racism and multiple sclerosis risk and outcomes in the USA
rationale: Topic 13A — Structural racism as biological driver. Reframes racial disparities from genetic to sociostructural causes through embodiment concept. Essential for equity-oriented work.

title: Racial and ethnic disparities in multiple sclerosis prevalence
rationale: Topic 13B — Kaiser Permanente SCAL prevalence study. MS prevalence similarly high in White and Black persons directly contradicting historical framing of MS as predominantly white disease.

title: Artificial intelligence and multiple sclerosis
rationale: Topic 14A — AI in MS clinical practice. Covers technical landscape of AI applications in MS while being honest about the gap between model performance and clinical deployment.

title: Integrating big data and artificial intelligence to predict progression in multiple sclerosis: challenges and the path forward
rationale: Topic 14B — Implementation gaps for MS AI. Addresses federated learning data governance explainable AI patient-centred design and regulatory pathways. Most relevant for learning systems work.

title: AI-driven reclassification of multiple sclerosis progression
rationale: Topic 15A — Data-driven disease reclassification. ML model on 8000+ patients identifying four-dimensional disease states directly challenging RRMS/SPMS/PPMS taxonomy.

title: Remyelination in the CNS: from biology to therapy
rationale: Topic 15B — Canonical remyelination biology paper. Establishes why spontaneous repair occurs why it fails in progressive MS and what OPC-based therapeutic strategies are being pursued.

title: Pathways to cures for multiple sclerosis: A research roadmap
rationale: Topic 16A — Original Cures Roadmap establishing Stop/Restore/End framework. Developed with 300+ people with MS input endorsed by 20+ organizations. Field's explicit statement of unmet needs.

title: The refined Pathways to Cures Research Roadmap for multiple sclerosis cures
rationale: Topic 16B — 2024 Roadmap update incorporating recent advances and extending to 30+ endorsing organizations. Adds data sharing regulatory engagement diversity and patient involvement recommendations.
